key_insight: The paper shows that a constrained LLM can safely assist finite element simulation by handling only parsing and geometry setup, while deterministic human-written FEniCS templates remain in control of the solver.
engineering_relevance: This makes natural-language-driven multi-physics analysis more practical for engineers by reducing setup effort without handing critical numerical logic to the model.
method: LLM converts prompts into structured JSON with retry-based validation
LLM generates Gmsh code only for non-catalog custom geometries
A deterministic dispatcher selects one of five fixed FEniCS/UFL templates
evidence_signal: The parser benchmark reached 100% final valid parses and 97.1% field-extraction accuracy, while custom geometry routing achieved 90% final success; template-layer validation matched benchmarks from sub-percent to about 2–5% error.
watch_item: The approach still depends on prompt/geometry validation, and custom-geometry failures can occur when the generated geometry is invalid.
paper_title: A Constrained Natural-Language Interface for Variational Multi-Physics Finite Element Simulations in FEniCS
